# From Regression to One-Way Analysis of Variance (ANOVA) {#oneway_anova}

[({{< meta texts.cb.initials >}} §11.4; {{< meta texts.wms.initials >}} §11.15)]{.small}

- So far, we modeled relationships with quantitative predictors

- Now we consider a *categorical predictor*


::: {.key-point}
Do different groups have different means?
:::

<h3>Set-up</h3>

- $Y_{ij}$, $i=1,\ldots,k,\; j=1,\ldots,n_i$ comes from group $i$ and IID observation $j$ in that group

- Total sample size is $n = \sum_{i=1}^k n_i$

- Compare the group means, $\mu_i = \Ex(Y_{ij})$, $i=1,\ldots,k$

---

## Model for one-way ANOVA

- Model:

$$
Y_{ij} = \mu_i + \varepsilon_{ij}
$$

- Assumptions:

$$
\varepsilon_{ij} \sim \Norm(0,\sigma^2), \quad \text{independent}
$$

- Each group has its own mean $\mu_i$

---

## Hypotheses

- We test:

$$
H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \cdots = \mu_k
$$

$$
H_a: \text{not all } \mu_i \text{ are equal}
$$

---

## Sample means

- Group means:

$$
\bar Y_i = \frac{1}{n_i} \sum_{j=1}^{n_i} Y_{ij}
$$

- Overall mean:

$$
\bar Y = \frac{1}{n} \sum_{i=1}^k \sum_{j=1}^{n_i} Y_{ij}
$$

---

## Total variability

- Total variation in the data:

$$
\text{SST}
=
\sum_{i=1}^k \sum_{j=1}^{n_i}
(Y_{ij} - \bar Y)^2
$$

- Measures how spread out all observations are

---

## Decomposition of variability

- We decompose total variation:

$$
\text{SST} = \text{SSB} + \text{SSW}
$$

- Between-group variation:

$$
\text{SSB}
=
\sum_{i=1}^k n_i (\bar Y_i - \bar Y)^2
$$

- Within-group variation:

$$
\text{SSW}
=
\sum_{i=1}^k \sum_{j=1}^{n_i}
(Y_{ij} - \bar Y_i)^2
$$

---

## Interpretation

- $\text{SSB}$:

  - measures differences between group means

- $\text{SSW}$:

  - measures variability within groups

- Key idea:

> Compare signal (between) vs noise (within)

---

## Degrees of freedom

- Between groups:

$$
k - 1
$$

- Within groups:

$$
n - k
$$

- Total:

$$
n - 1
$$

---

## Mean squares

- Define:

$$
\text{MSB} = \frac{\text{SSB}}{k-1}
$$

$$
\text{MSW} = \frac{\text{SSW}}{n-k}
$$

- $\text{MSW}$ estimates $\sigma^2$

---

## F-statistic

- Compare variability:

$$
F = \frac{\text{MSB}}{\text{MSW}}
$$

- Under $H_0$:

$$
F \sim F_{k-1,\,n-k}
$$

---

## Interpretation of the F-test

- Large $F$:

  - strong evidence that means differ

- Small $F$:

  - no evidence of differences

- Important:

> ANOVA detects differences, not which groups differ

---

## ANOVA table

| Source        | Sum of Squares | df      | Mean Square |
|---------------|----------------|---------|-------------|
| Between groups| SSB            | $k-1$   | MSB         |
| Within groups | SSW            | $n-k$   | MSW         |
| Total         | SST            | $n-1$   |             |

---

## Big picture

- One-way ANOVA compares multiple means

- Uses variance decomposition:

  - between-group variation  
  - within-group variation  

- Leads to a single global test

---

## Next steps

- How ANOVA connects to regression

- Dummy variables and design matrices

- Identifying which groups differ
